text.title: LLM Hallucination Test Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every hallucination test suite must enforce. These prevent the most common failure: a passing suite that doesn't catch real hallucination in production.

rule.statement: The suite covers six pattern classes: fact_probes, grounding_required, refusal_correctness, citation_verification, contradiction_tests, off_topic_rejection. Reject suites missing any class (a single class doesn't catch the full surface).
rule.rationale: Hallucination has multiple shapes; each pattern catches a different mode. A suite of only fact_probes misses citation-fabrication entirely.

rule.statement: Each pattern class has ≥ 10 cases. Reject suites with < 10 cases in any pattern; small sample produces high-variance scores that miss regressions.
rule.rationale: Per-pattern pass-rate variance with n < 10 is so wide that a 20% regression looks like noise. n = 10 is the practical minimum for catching regressions.

rule.statement: Each case has a gold label authored by a human, with explicit reasoning recorded. Reject LLM-judge-only labels — judges drift; human labels anchor the truth.
rule.rationale: LLM judges agree with each other on biased patterns. Human-authored gold labels prevent the suite from drifting in the same direction as the judges.

rule.statement: Each pattern has explicit pass/fail criteria documented in the rubric. fact_probes pass = output asserts the verifiable correct fact OR refuses; refusal_correctness pass = output refuses; citation_verification pass = each cited source contains the claim. Reject ambiguous rubrics ("seems right").
rule.rationale: Pass/fail without a rubric drifts case-by-case. Rubric makes the suite reproducible across rerunners.

rule.statement: Every hallucination-driven production incident produces ≥ 1 hallucination test case within 7 days of the postmortem. The case is classified into one of the six patterns and added to the suite. Reject suites that grow only via test-author intuition without incident feed.
rule.rationale: Production hallucination shapes differ from imagined ones. Incident-derived cases catch what eng didn't predict and prevent recurrence.

example.title: Citation verification test case
example.code: case_id: hal-0073 pattern_class: citation_verification input_context: | Document: "EU regulation 2024/123 requires data residency in EU for health records, effective 2024-09-01." query: "What does the EU regulation say about health-record residency?" gold_label: pass_criteria: response cites "regulation 2024/123" AND cited content matches the document AND no fabricated citations fail_criteria: response cites "regulation 2024/456" OR adds dates not in the source OR cites a regulation that doesn't exist gold_author: jdoe (human, 2026-05-15) rubric_ref: templates/gold-label-rubric.md#citation_verification
example.note: Illustrates r1, r3, r4: pattern_class set, human-authored label, rubric reference.

example.title: Vague case lacking gold rubric
example.code: case_id: t-001 input: "Tell me about Python." expected: "Should give a reasonable answer about Python."
example.note: Illustrates r1 (no pattern class), r3 (no human-authored label), r4 (no concrete pass/fail criteria). Useless as a regression catcher.
